_description: Threat responses and recurring vulnerabilities — maintained by Cyber-Agent and QA-Agent

threat_model.response_protocol: Critical + High Probability → STOP pipeline, report to user immediately

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: JWT stored in localStorage exposes to XSS
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: SB frontend stores JWT in localStorage. Any XSS vulnerability allows token theft and full account takeover.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Migrate to httpOnly cookies with SameSite=Strict. Add Content-Security-Policy header. Sanitize all user-rendered content.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: No rate limiting on SSE streaming endpoint
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: POST /assistant/user-input supports SSE streaming but has no per-user rate limit. Attacker can open many concurrent streams, exhausting server memory.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Add per-user concurrent stream limit (max 3). Add request rate limit (20 req/min per user). Implement SSE keepalive timeout (5 min max).

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: API key prefix sk_live_ is predictable
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: API keys use sk_live_ prefix making them easy to identify in logs and code. While not a direct vulnerability, it increases exposure risk.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Ensure API keys are never logged. Add key rotation mechanism. Monitor for leaked keys in public repos.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: Security review (cyber-agent) запланирован после backend/frontend — слишком поздно для API key management системы
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: По структуре pipeline, s3.5-cyber и s5.5-cyber-audit стоят после architect и после backend/frontend соответственно. Для задачи с API key management (шифрование, хранение sensitive data в файловой системе, audit log) это означает, что архитектурные решения по шифрованию и хранению принимаются без security input. Cyber-agent на s3.5 получает уже готовую архитектуру и может только реагировать, а не проектировать.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Для задач с тегами [sensitive-data, encryption, auth, payments]: переместить первый cyber-agent вызов на s2.5 — между PM и Architect. Cyber формирует "Security Requirements Brief" (алгоритм шифрования, key derivation, storage constraints, audit requirements), который Architect получает как обязательный input наравне с PM stories. Конкретно для API key management: AES-256-GCM с PBKDF2/Argon2 для key derivation, запрет хранения plaintext даже временно, audit log должен быть append-only с HMAC integrity check.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: No Rate Limiting or Per-User Request Throttling on Points Earn/Redemption Endpoints
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: s3.5-cyber (8.8/10) identified that the existing stack had no pre-existing rate limiting middleware, and that loyalty earn/redemption endpoints (POST /api/loyalty/earn, POST /api/loyalty/redeem) are high-value targets for replay attacks and rapid-fire abuse. Without throttling, an attacker with a valid session token can loop redemption calls to drain points balances or trigger race conditions on the two-table transactional update (loyalty_balance + loyalty_ledger).
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Add per-user rate limit rules on earn/redeem endpoints (e.g., max 10 earn calls per minute per salon_id+client_id pair, max 3 redemption calls per minute). Implement idempotency keys on POST /api/loyalty/redeem so duplicate requests within a time window return the cached result rather than executing twice. These controls should be specified in the API contract stage and enforced in middleware before business logic executes.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: Client-Side Currency Icon Injection via Unvalidated Dropdown Values
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: The s4-cyber step identified that currency icon selection driven by dropdown values may be injected client-side without server-side validation, allowing potentially unvalidated values to reach the icon rendering layer in CostCounter.tsx.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Validate selected dropdown values against a strict server-side or compile-time allowlist of supported currency codes before resolving icons. Never trust client-supplied enum values for asset/icon lookup without sanitization.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: Unvalidated Currency Value from Dropdown Passed to Storage
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: The Cyber-Agent (s4-cyber) identified that currency values selected from the dropdown UI were being passed to storage without server-side validation. The value flows from UI selection directly into persistence layer, trusting client input.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Validate currency value server-side against an explicit whitelist of supported ISO currency codes before any storage or state update. Reject and return 400 for any value not in the allowed set. Frontend constraints are UX, not security.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: localStorage Menu State Requires Input Sanitization
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: The s4-cyber agent flagged that persisting collapsed/expanded menu state in localStorage without sanitization introduces a potential XSS/injection vector. Even though the risk was rated Low, client-side storage of UI state is a common overlooked attack surface in shell/layout components.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: When reading menu state from localStorage, always parse through a strict schema validator (e.g., only accept boolean or enum values). Never interpolate raw localStorage values into DOM attributes or innerHTML. Use a whitelist approach: `const isCollapsed = localStorage.getItem('menuCollapsed') === 'true'` rather than direct assignment.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: Tooltip Data Source Exposure Risk in Metric Boxes
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: s4-cyber flagged that displaying internal data source identifiers (API endpoints, internal service names) directly in metric box tooltips could expose sensitive infrastructure topology to end users. The risk was classified Low but warrants design consideration.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Tooltip content should use human-readable labels mapped from internal identifiers — never expose raw endpoint URLs, service names, or internal identifiers. Maintain a display-name mapping layer between data source config and UI rendering. s3.5-cyber-reeval-1 confirmed the implementation passed re-evaluation at 22.3s, indicating the frontend agent applied this correctly.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: Dark Mode Preference Stored in Client-Side State Without SSR Hydration Guard
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: The cyber agent (s4-cyber) flagged that theme preference stored only in React state or localStorage creates a hydration mismatch risk — the server renders with a default theme while the client immediately switches, causing a flash of unstyled/wrong-theme content (FOUC) on load.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Wrap theme reads in a `suppressHydrationWarning` attribute on `<html>`, or inject a blocking inline script in `_document`/`layout.tsx` that sets the `data-theme` attribute before React hydrates. Use `next-themes` library which handles this pattern correctly out of the box. Never read localStorage in component initial state — always default to system preference via `prefers-color-scheme` media query as the SSR-safe fallback.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: Unbounded Data Rendering Without Scroll Cap Exposes Performance Attack Surface
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: s4-cyber identified that the Daily Breakdown (All Time) table in the Costs page rendered all rows without any virtualization or scroll boundary. An attacker or misconfigured data source could inject thousands of rows, causing browser thread exhaustion and potential DoS-level UI freeze.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Implemented a scroll boundary after 20 rows (max-h + overflow-y-auto or a virtualized list). For production hardening, pair with row-count server-side limits and sanitize any user-controlled field values before render to prevent XSS via injected cost labels or category strings.

recurring_vulnerabilities.title: Missing Row-Level Data Sanitization Before Render in Cost Tables
recurring_vulnerabilities.description: s3.5-cyber-reeval confirmed that while the scroll fix addressed the original unbounded rendering concern, no row-level sanitization was applied to cost data fields before they reached the DOM. Fields like category names, vendor labels, or description strings sourced from external APIs could carry injected payloads.
recurring_vulnerabilities.mitigation: Apply sanitization (e.g., DOMPurify or equivalent) to all string fields rendered in the Daily Breakdown rows, particularly any fields that may originate from external billing sources, imports, or user-editable metadata. Treat cost data as untrusted at the render boundary.

api_endpoints: Rate limiting on all public endpoints
IDOR checks — always verify ownership before returning data
JWT expiration and refresh token rotation
frontend: XSS prevention — sanitize all user-rendered content
Sensitive data never in localStorage (use httpOnly cookies)
infrastructure: Secrets in env vars only, never in code
Database backups daily with tested restore
Audit logging for all admin actions
152_fz_compliance: PII stored on Russian servers (Timeweb) only
Data deletion on user request within 30 days
Access logging for all PII reads
